message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To examine coroners’ Prevention of Future Deaths reports (PFDs) to identify deaths involving SARS-CoV-2 that coroners deemed preventable.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Consecutive case series.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Setting</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>England and Wales.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Participants</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Patients reported in 510 PFDs dated between 01 January 2020 and 28 June 2021, collected from the UK’s Courts and Tribunals Judiciary website using web scraping to create an openly available database,
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://preventabledeathstracker.net/">https://preventabledeathstracker.net/</jats:ext-link>
                    .
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main outcome measures</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Concerns reported by coroners.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Public and Patient Involvement</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Patients and members of the public were not involved in this study.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>SARS-CoV-2 was involved in 23 deaths reported by coroners in PFDs. Twelve deaths were indirectly related to the COVID-19 pandemic, defined as those that were not medically caused by SARS-CoV-2, but were associated with mitigation measures. In 11 cases the coroner explicitly reported that COVID-19 had directly caused death. There was geographical variation in the reporting of PFDs; most (39%) were written by coroners in the North-West of England. The coroners raised 56 concerns, problems in communication being the most common (30%), followed by failure to follow protocols (23%). Organizations in the National Health Service (NHS) were sent the most PFDs (51%), followed by the Government (26%), but responses to PFDs by these organizations were poor.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    PFDs contain a rich source of information on preventable deaths that has previously been difficult to examine systematically. Our openly available tool (
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://preventabledeathstracker.net/">https://preventabledeathstracker.net/</jats:ext-link>
                    ) streamlines this process and has identified many concerns raised by coroners that should be addressed during the Government’s inquiry into the handling of the COVID-19 pandemic, so that mistakes made are less likely to be repeated.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Study protocol pre-registration</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://osf.io/bfypc/">https://osf.io/bfypc/</jats:ext-link>
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Summary box</jats:title>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>What is already known about this subject?</jats:title>
                    <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>The UK Government has stated that there will be a public inquiry into the handling of the COVID-19 pandemic, to learn lessons for future pandemics.</jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>Coroners in England and Wales have a duty to report and communicate information about the deaths they investigate when the coroner believes that action should be taken to prevent future deaths.</jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>These reports, called Prevention of Future Death reports (PFDs), had not yet been systematically analysed to identify deaths that occurred during the COVID-19 pandemic.</jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                    </jats:list>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>What are the new findings?</jats:title>
                    <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>
                          We created the Preventable Deaths Database (
                          <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://preventabledeathstracker.net/">https://preventabledeathstracker.net/</jats:ext-link>
                          ) using web scraping to systematically assess PFDs published on the Courts and Tribunal Judiciary website.
                        </jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>Between 01 January 2020 and 28 June 2021, one in 20 (4.5%, n=23) PFDs that were published by coroners involved SARS-CoV-2.</jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>Coroners raised many concerns about the care of patients in hospitals, care homes, and people in the community during the COVID-19 pandemic, which require action to prevent future deaths.</jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                    </jats:list>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>How might it affect clinical practice in the foreseeable future?</jats:title>
                    <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>Preventable deaths that occurred during the COVID-19 pandemic should be referred to the coroner so that an inquest can be performed and a PFD issued, highlightng actions that could be avoided in improving the handling of future pandemics in both the UK and elsewhere.</jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>
                          Our tool,
                          <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://preventabledeathstracker.net/">https://preventabledeathstracker.net/</jats:ext-link>
                          , can be used by others to examine preventable deaths in England and Wales, and to identify signals for quality improvement to reduce avoidable harms in clinical practice.
                        </jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                    </jats:list>
                  </jats:sec>
                </jats:sec>
title: Preventable deaths from SARS-CoV-2 in England and Wales: a systematic case series of coroners’ reports during the COVID-19 pandemic

reference.unstructured: WHO Coronavirus (COVID-19) Dashboard. World Health Organization, 2021. https://covid19.who.int
Cox AR , Ferner R. Tramadol: repeated prescriptions and repeated warnings. BMJ Evid Based Med 2021 Mar 8; bmjebm-2020-111661.

Ains A , Heneghan C , Aronson JK , DeVito NJ , Richards GC . Deaths from cardiovascular disease involving anticoagulants: a systematic synthesis of coroners’ case reports. BJGP Open 2021; BJGPO.2021.0150.

Odgers L , Aronson JK , Richards GC . Preventable deaths involving cyclists in England and Wales: solutions to improve their safety. Global Centre on Healthcare and Urbanisation. https://www.gchu.org.uk/2021/09/gchu-report-preventable-deaths-involving-cyclists-in-england-and-wales (2021).
Kendrick D , Agius R , McKee M , Robertson J , Sewell H , Stewart M. COVID-19: Learning lessons from the deaths of our colleagues. BJGP Life 2020; https://bjgplife.com/2020/05/18/gp-deaths/.

Swift B , Heneghan C , Aronson JK , Howard D , Richards. Preventable deaths from SARS-CoV-2 in England and Wales: a systematic analysis of coroners’ case reports. OSF Registries 29 June 2021; https://osf.io/ej6ks
Judiciary. Prevention of Future Deaths. Courts and Tribunals Judiciary 2021; https://www.judiciary.uk/subject/prevention-of-future-deaths/.
Richards, G.C. Preventable Deaths Tracker. 2021. https://preventabledeathstracker.net
